content.text: This References and Compliance Library consolidates all authoritative sources that underpin the policies, procedures, and practices outlined in this Principal's Handbook. The library serves multiple critical functions: For Regulatory Compliance: Provides direct access to Indonesian national education regulations, enabling schools to verify compliance with Ministry of Education requirements, Yayasan obligations, and national curriculum mandates. For Accreditation Readiness: Links to CIS and WASC standards documentation, supporting self-study preparation and evidence compilation for accreditation cycles. For Cambridge Centre Operations: References Cambridge International's official handbooks, guides, and policy documents essential for maintaining Centre status and examination integrity. For Professional Practice: Curates best-practice resources for English as an Additional Language (EAL), Special Educational Needs (SEN), and Multi-Tiered System of Supports (MTSS) implementation. For Evidence Management: The Reference Index (Appendix 20.1) tracks version numbers, update dates, and responsible officers to ensure schools maintain current documentation.

content.text: This index maps each handbook section to its primary regulatory and standards references: \=\> TABLO \<=
The References and Compliance Library undergoes a systematic review to ensure currency and accuracy: July (Start of Academic Year):

items: Review all Indonesian regulations for updates published during the previous year
Verify Cambridge Handbook updates and incorporate changes
Update CIS/WASC framework references if new versions are released
content.title: 20.9.1 Annual Review Cycle

content.text: January (Mid-Year Review):

items: Monitor for mid-year regulatory changes (particularly Ministry circulars)
Review EAL/SEN resources for new publications

content.text: Ongoing Monitoring:

items: Subscribe to JDIH Kemendikbud notification system
Track CIS/WASC communications to member schools

content.text: All reference updates are tracked in the Reference Index (Appendix 20.1) with:
The following evidence demonstrates effective reference management and compliance awareness:
For ease of access during daily operations, the following Quick Reference Cards are maintained at each school: QRC-A: Key Indonesian Contacts
QRC-B: Cambridge Key Contacts
QRC-C: Emergency Compliance Contacts
